editionData.title: Daily Brief for May 4, 2026: Global Headlines, Markets, Hacker News, Product Hunt
editionData.description: A daily brief for May 4, 2026 covering global headlines, market closes, Hacker News, and Product Hunt.
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: Global Demand for Rare Earth Minerals Is Intensifying Criminal Threats in the Amazon Rainforest
headlines.summary: Global demand for critical minerals, used to build drones and electric cars, is surging, setting of a new wave of criminality in the world’s largest rainforest.

headlines.title: Crack Down? Close Ranks? U.S. Cartel Case Puts Mexican Leaders on the Spot.
headlines.summary: The indictment of a Mexican governor has spotlighted corruption, strained cross-border relations and handed President Claudia Sheinbaum a thorny choice.

headlines.title: What to Know About Elections in West Bengal and Other Indian States
headlines.summary: Vote counting starts on Monday after over 154 million people participated in elections for state legislatures last month. The contests can tilt the balance of power for the whole country.

headlines.title: How A.I. Is Transforming China’s Entertainment Industry
headlines.summary: A.I.-generated microdramas have taken off. Celebrities have threatened legal action against the use of their likeness, while actors say jobs have dried up.

headlines.title: ‘Wartime Relic’ Explodes Under Campfire in Austria, Injuring Five Children
headlines.summary: The children, aged 10 to 14, were taken to a hospital for treatment after the explosion in Upper Austria, the police said.

headlines.title: Iran warns Trump’s escort mission in Strait of Hormuz violates ceasefire
Three dead in suspected hantavirus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
French left-wing’s Melenchon says he will run for president in 2027
Trump says US will ‘help free up’ ships stuck in Hormuz Strait
Investigators say Trump assassination suspect shot officer at press gala
Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
headlines.summary: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

headlines.title: Gas prices went up more than 30 cents a gallon last week. How high could they go?
headlines.summary: U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.

headlines.title: Pellet found in Secret Service agent's vest links suspect to WHCD attack, Pirro says
headlines.summary: The pellet "definitively" links the suspect, Cole Tomas Allen, to the attack, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro told CNN on Sunday.

headlines.title: 'I just want to see her again' says son of imprisoned Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi
headlines.summary: On Thursday, authorities in Myanmar claimed they had transferred Nobel laureate Aung San Suu Kyi from prison to house arrest. Her son Kim Aris spoke to NPR about his doubts about the regime's account.

headlines.title: He recorded his quest for tariff refunds. It shows why billions may never get repaid
headlines.summary: After the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, Richard Brown began lining up the paperwork he needed to get his refund. Experts say many businesses may never get their money back.

headlines.title: Japanese PM reaffirms intention to revise Constitution
headlines.summary: TOKYO -- Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, on Sunday, the country's Constitution Memorial Day, reaffirmed her intention to revise the Constitution, local media reported.

headlines.title: US May Day rallies draw tens of thousands nationwide
headlines.summary: NEW YORK -- Tens of thousands of people took to the streets across the United States on Friday to join May Day rallies against the US-Israeli war on Iran and defend labor and immigrant rights.

headlines.title: BP criticized over surging profits following Iran war
headlines.summary: The United Kingdom-based oil and gas giant BP is facing criticism after reporting its profits more than doubled in the first quarter of the year, following a spike in oil prices since the Iran war began.

headlines.title: UAE exits oil groupings, eyes more output
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates is set to substantially ramp up its oil production capacity after quitting the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and the broader OPEC+ alliance effective Friday, making a pivotal shift at a time when the war between the United States, Israel and Iran has triggered an energy crisis and roiled the global economy.

headlines.title: 7 OPEC+ countries to increase oil production
headlines.summary: VIENNA -- OPEC said in a communique on Sunday that seven OPEC+ countries decided to increase their oil production cap by 188,000 barrels per day in June, in response to the withdrawal of the United Arab Emirates from the bloc.

headlines.title: Two security staff members arrested after video shows them assaulting tribal man at Madhya Pradesh coal plant
headlines.summary: Two security staff arrested for assaulting a tribal man at a Madhya Pradesh coal plant after viral video surfaced.

headlines.title: 284 companies to showcase defence tech at North Tech Symposium 2026
headlines.summary: Join 284 companies at the North Tech Symposium 2026, showcasing innovative defence technologies to enhance national security and military capabilities.

headlines.title: 28 injured as bus overturns in Himachal Pradesh's Kangra
headlines.summary: A bus overturn in Himachal Pradesh's Kangra injures 28, with preliminary reports suggesting driver heart attack caused the accident.

headlines.title: Silence, the court is in session
headlines.summary: Explore the opacity in judicial accountability following Allahabad High Court judge Varma's resignation amid corruption allegations and scrutiny.

headlines.title: PM Modi, External Affairs Minister on major diplomatic outreach in May
headlines.summary: PM Modi and Jaishankar embark on a busy diplomatic summer, enhancing India's global outreach and strategic partnerships.
